title: Verifier pack (v1)
description: Canonical v1 schema for a verifier pack: the mandatory mirror of a skill. Every skill referenced by a run contract MUST ship a sibling verifier pack. A pack is a closed list of binary-observable assertions that fail loudly when the skill's declared behaviour is not produced.

skill.description: Skill id this pack mirrors. MUST resolve to a skill manifest in the cohort; catalog CI fails on missing siblings.

version.description: Semver of the pack itself; bumped independently of the skill version.

schema_version.description: Semver of THIS verifier-pack schema. v1 packs declare 1.x.y.

description.description: One-sentence summary of what the pack verifies.

fingerprint.description: sha256:<hex> content-hash of the canonicalised pack body. Generated; not author-supplied.

setup.description: Commands run once before any verifier executes.

teardown.description: Cleanup commands run after all verifiers complete (always run, even on failure).

verifiers.description: Ordered list of verifier assertions. Each is a binary-observable check that either passes, fail_louds, mark_untrusted, or returns inconclusive.

verifier_kind.description: Canonical kinds. Stable across pack versions. `priming_active` (added in 1.1.0) asserts the cell's rendered system prompt contains the canonical anti-confabulation priming block, hash-matched by SHA-256 over the priming-block region; see doctrine/patterns/anti-confabulation-priming.md. `custom` is the escape hatch and MUST carry a description that explains why a standard kind would not fit.

failure_mode.description: fail_loud = run REJECTED; mark_untrusted = outputs flagged provisional; inconclusive = pack-execution itself errored (loudest possible).

id.description: Optional snake_case id for cross-reference inside the pack.

description.description: Human-readable statement of what is being asserted.

command.description: Shell-or-program invocation that runs the verifier. May reference environment variables like ${OUTPUT_DIR}; resolution rules are executor-defined.

expected_exit.description: Expected exit code. Null = exit code is not asserted (stdout/stderr/artefacts will be).

expected_stdout_contains.description: Required substring in stdout. Null = stdout is not asserted.

expected_stderr_contains.description: Required substring in stderr. Null = stderr is not asserted.

expected_artefacts.description: Relative paths that MUST exist after the verifier runs. Empty array means the verifier produces no artefacts (and that absence is intentional, not implied).

severity.description: Pre-fail-mode triage. fatal = run rejected on failure; error = degraded; warning = log only. Pairs with failure_mode.

timeout_seconds.description: Per-verifier wall-clock cap. If exceeded the result is `inconclusive` (loudest).

preconditions.description: Verifier ids that must pass before this one runs. A precondition failure causes a `skip` — and skips are themselves audited.
